rulings.Case Title: SECURITY CREDIT SERVICES, LLC VS. GLENN A. KINARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Assigning Sums Due Or That Will Be Due Judgment Debtor From Rental Of Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 16, 2024 line 1. ASSIGNEE PERRYVILLE RECOVERY CORPORATION ( C/O BREWER & BREWER LLP ) NOTICE OF MOTION FOR ORDER ASSIGNING SUMS DUE OR THAT WILL BE DUE JUDGMENT DEBTOR FROM RENTAL OF REAL PROPERTY is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SFHA HOUSING CORPORATION VS. JOHN DOE I ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 16, 2024 line 8. DEFENDANT CLEVELAND REED HEARING ON CLAIM OF RIGHT TO POSSESSION Hearing Required. Claimant to present evidence of occupancy and tenancy at the hearing, including but not limited to (1) a copy of a government issued ID listing the address of the premises; (2) utility bills for the premises in claimant's name; (3) receipts for rent payment; cancelled checks; regular bank withdrawals in the amount of monthly rent, etc. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JALASAI BAPU, LLC VS. KADIO EMMANUEL ASSEMAN JUS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant To Appear For Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 16, 2024 line 9. PLAINTIFF JALASAI BAPU, LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Plaintiff will be severely prejudiced in its ability to prepare for trial without Defendants deposition. Therefore, Defendant is ordered to appear for deposition on a 5-day notice and trial is continued to November 4, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
